entries.definition:
Qur’an: أَعْجَمِىّ ʾaʿjamiyy three times and أَعْجَمِين ʾaʿjamīn once.
أَعْجَمِىّ ʾaʿjamiyy [quasi-act. part., pl. أَعْجَمِين ʾaʿjamīn] foreign, in a foreign tongue, non-Arab, non-Arabic (41:44) وَلَوْ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ قُرْءَانًا أَعْجَمِيًّا لَّقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا فُصِّلَتْ ءَايَـٰتُهُۥٓ ءَأَعْجَمِىٌّ وَعَرَبِىٌّ
had We made it a foreign Qur’an, they would have said, ‘If only its verses were made clear!–what!? Foreign speech and an Arab [audience/messenger]?’
entries.definition_english:
Qur’an: أَعْجَمِىّ ʾaʿjamiyy three times and أَعْجَمِين ʾaʿjamīn once.
أَعْجَمِىّ ʾaʿjamiyy [quasi-act. part., pl. أَعْجَمِين ʾaʿjamīn] foreign, in a foreign tongue, non-Arab, non-Arabic (41:44) وَلَوْ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ قُرْءَانًا أَعْجَمِيًّا لَّقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا فُصِّلَتْ ءَايَـٰتُهُۥٓ ءَأَعْجَمِىٌّ وَعَرَبِىٌّ
had We made it a foreign Qur’an, they would have said, ‘If only its verses were made clear!–what!? Foreign speech and an Arab [audience/messenger]?’

entries.definition:
ʿ–d–d gear; group, number, to count, to number, to reckon, to enumerate, an appointed time; plenty of; to be reckoned with; to get ready, readiness. Of this root, 13 forms occur 57 times in the Qur’an: عَدَّ ʿadda seven times; عَدَّدَ ʿaddada once; أَعَدَّ ʾaʿadda 16 times; عُدْتَ ʿuidda four times; تَعْتَدُّ taʿtadd once; عَدّ ʿadd twice; عَدَد ʿadad six times; عِدَّة ʿiddatun 11 times; عُدَّة ʿuddatun once; مَعْدُود maʿdūd once; مَعْدُودَة maʿdūdatun three times and مَعْدُودَات maʿdūdāt three times.
عَدَّ ʿadda u [v. trans.] 1 to count (14:34) وَإِن تَعُدُّوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَا
if you were to count God’s blessings you could never number them 2 to consider, to take for, to count among (38:62) وَقَالُوا۟ مَا لَنَا لَا نَرَىٰ رِجَالًا كُنَّا نَعُدُّهُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَشْرَارِ
and they will say, ‘How is it that we do not see [certain] men we used to count among the wicked?’

عَدَّدَ ʿaddada [v. II. trans.] to enumerate, to cause to increase, to compound (104:1–2) ٱلَّذِى جَمَعَ مَالًا وَعَدَّدَهُۥ
he who amasses riches, counting/compounding them [all the time].

أَعَدَّ ʾaʿadda [v. IV trans.] to prepare (33:29) فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْمُحْسِنَـٰتِ مِنكُنَّ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
for God has prepared for those of you who do good a great reward.

تَعْتَدُّ taʿtadd [imperf. of v. VIII, trans. ٱعْتَدَّ iʿtadad] [jur.] (of the waiting period divorced women have to observe) to reckon, to count/to demand fulfilment (33:49) فَمَا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ عِدَّةٍ تَعْتَدُّونَهَا
then you have no right to exact of them a waiting period which you calculate (or, the accomplishment of which you exact).

عَدّ ʿadd [v. n., used adverbially for intensification] reckoning, counting; to the last one (19:94) لَّقَدْ أَحْصَىٰهُمْ وَعَدَّهُمْ عَدًّا
He has tallied them up, and counted them to the last one.

604

604
